tagline: Single-ply reflective membrane — lower cooling costs, longer roof life.
metaDescription: TPO single-ply roofing in Los Angeles County for commercial and residential flat roofs. Energy-efficient cool-roof systems. Free inspections. CA Lic. #1109337.
intro: TPO (thermoplastic polyolefin) is a single-ply reflective membrane that's become the dominant commercial low-slope roofing material — and it's increasingly popular on residential flat roofs and additions. Palm Crest Roofing installs TPO in mechanically fastened, adhered, and induction-welded systems across LA County.
benefits: Reflective white surface cuts cooling loads 20%+
Title 24 and ENERGY STAR cool-roof compliant
Heat-welded seams are stronger than the membrane itself
Lightweight — often suitable over existing roofs
GAF, Carlisle, Johns Manville, Firestone systems

process.title: Inspection
process.desc: Assess deck, slope, drainage, and existing roof. TPO can sometimes be installed over a prepared existing roof.

process.title: Prep
process.desc: Tear off or prepare substrate. Install insulation and cover board per project requirements.

process.title: Membrane Installation
process.desc: TPO rolled out and secured by mechanical fasteners, adhesive, or induction welding per spec.

process.title: Heat-Welded Seams
process.desc: Every seam is heat-welded with a hot-air gun — creating a bond stronger than the membrane itself.

process.title: Flashings & Terminations
process.desc: Walls, penetrations, and edges are flashed with TPO-coated metal and sealed.

faqs.q: What's the difference between TPO and PVC?
faqs.a: Both are single-ply reflective membranes. PVC is more chemical-resistant (good for restaurants with grease exhaust); TPO is slightly cheaper and equally durable for most applications.

faqs.q: Can TPO be installed over my existing roof?
faqs.a: Often yes — with proper prep, insulation, and cover board. We'll inspect and tell you whether a recover or full tear-off is appropriate.

faqs.q: How long do TPO seams last?
faqs.a: Properly heat-welded seams are designed to last the life of the membrane (15–25+ years). They don't rely on adhesives that can fail.
